title: 3-in-1 Fire Pit Bench
summary: A curved, trapezoidal fire-pit bench section built from 2x6 slats on angled 2x4 leg sets. Build several to ring a fire pit; each is all 15° cuts.
description:
One section of a modular fire-pit seating ring: a trapezoidal bench, wider at the back than the front, so several of them arc around a fire pit into a hexagon or octagon. The 3-in-1 name comes from the system — build them as benches, or top some as a table, or box one in for storage. This plan is one bench.

The whole thing is 15° cuts. The leg-set pieces are cut at 15° so the two ends of the bench splay to form the trapezoid, and the two leg sets are mirror images of each other. Lay out one leg set, screw it together at the overlaps with 2-3/4" exterior deck screws (add exterior glue for strength), then build the second as a mirror.

Because it lives outside near heat and weather, use exterior deck screws and a rot-resistant or treated species, and space the 2x6 seat slats with an even gap so rain drains and the seat dries. Twelve 2x6 slats top one bench. Cut carefully and check that the two leg sets match before you deck it — a mismatch throws off how the sections meet in the ring.

tools.note: Every cut is 15° — a miter saw makes them repeatable.

cutList.note: Long point to long point; both ends 15°, NOT parallel.
Long point to long point; both ends 15°, NOT parallel.
Long point to short point; both ends 15°, parallel.
Long point to short point; both ends 15°, parallel.
Long point to short point; both ends 15°, parallel.

steps.title: Build the first leg set
steps.body: Lay out one leg set and attach the pieces together at their overlaps with 2-3/4" exterior deck screws. Add exterior wood glue at the joints for strength. The 15° cuts give the trapezoidal splay that lets sections ring a fire pit.

steps.title: Build the mirror leg set
steps.body: Build the second leg set exactly the same, but mirrored. Confirm the two sets match before decking — a mismatch throws off how the bench meets its neighbors in the ring.

steps.title: Deck the seat
steps.body: Place the twelve 2x6 boards on top and attach, spacing the gaps evenly. Screw down with 2-3/4" screws and glue. The gaps let rain drain so the seat dries between uses. Let cedar silver or finish for exterior use.

images.alt: Trapezoidal fire-pit bench section built from 2x6 slats on angled legs
